message:
fix(dataflow): replace unwrap() calls with safe node access in graph wiring

The connect() and flush_debounce() paths used unwrap() on HashMap::get_mut
after existence was verified earlier in the flow, but concurrent modifications
or future refactors could make those assumptions brittle.

Now:
- connect(): Use if-let guards instead of unwrap() for node input/output wiring
- flush_debounce(): Use let-else (continue) instead of unwrap() for node lookup

Behavior is unchanged for well-formed graphs but avoids panics if node
removal races with wiring in future concurrent dataflow scenarios.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
